futureLaneSignals.quote: Knight Foundation Fellowship in Healthcare Technology Innovation - Through the generous support of the John S. and James L. Knight Foundation, Baptist Health Innovations has launched the Knight Foundation Fellowship for Healthcare Technology Innovation. This competitive fellowship is designed to attract, train and empower healthcare visionaries and entrepreneurs to develop a diverse and connected community of innovators at Baptist Health and in South Florida.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Baptist Health Innovations runs the Knight Foundation Fellowship in Healthcare Technology Innovation — an entrepreneurship/innovation fellowship, not a clinical/GME fellowship.
futureLaneSignals.limitations: Innovation/entrepreneurship fellowship, not ACGME clinical fellowship; audience and eligibility not stated. System-level scope, not campus-specific.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship targets healthcare visionaries/entrepreneurs for technology innovation, not medical students, residents, fellows, or attending clinical practice; does not map cleanly onto USCEHub Tiers 1–3.

futureLaneSignals.quote: South Florida residency (applicant must currently reside in one of these four South Florida counties: Miami-Dade County, Broward County, Palm Beach County or Monroe County).

futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Employment-based eligibility consistent with Tier 3 working-professional fellowship.
